title: eriogonum corymbosum var. thompsoniae
intro: Eriogonum corymbosum var. thompsoniae, also known as Thompson's buckwheat or desert buckwheat, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum corymbosum var. thompsoniae can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
